key_insight: The paper turns discrete topological constraints like Betti numbers into differentiable Hodge-spectral penalties, making topology-aware optimization tractable for graphs and point clouds.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a smoother, geometry-aware loss for design problems where preserving holes or connectivity matters, with gradients that are easier to optimize than persistent-homology losses.
method: Builds soft graph and soft clique-complex Hodge Laplacians that relax homological constraints.
Uses low-pass spectral objectives via heat kernels, resolvent filters, and polynomial Laplacian moments.
Recovers the usual Hodge Laplacian in the hard-limit active subcomplex while remaining differentiable in the soft regime.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that on point clouds the proposed losses produce more spatially distributed gradients, smoother scale-normalized behavior under persistence-pairing changes, and geometry-aware updates than persistent-homology-based losses.
watch_item: The surrogate relies on spectral relaxations, so choosing filter scales and ensuring the soft-to-hard limit matches the target topology may be sensitive in practice.
paper_title: Hodge Spectral Surrogates for Topology-Constrained Optimization
